executive_summary: Whole-body and dexterous manipulation see strong advances via hierarchical world models, symmetry-aware bimanual policies, and tactile/force-aware control for humanoids and multifingered hands.

Vision-Language-Action models are being re-architected around atomic skills, mixtures-of-experts, temporal memory, and efficient fine-tuning/quantization/edge-cloud inference for robust long-horizon manipulation.

Several works tackle long-horizon mobile and bimanual manipulation with structured task graphs, pose-centered affordances, and robust planning/execution under clutter, occlusion, and contact-rich dynamics.
Safety, monitoring, and robustness emerge as key themes, with conformal world-model monitors for bimanual failures, safety-guaranteed surgical policies, and residual control for dynamics shifts.
Tactile and visuo-tactile sensing are leveraged for contact-rich tasks, grasp stability, disassembly, and force selection, while new gripper and teleoperation platforms broaden practical deployment.
Surveys and data/learning engines assess and scale robotic foundation models, highlighting gaps between benchmark success and industry-grade readiness in manipulation contexts.

institution_matches.title: SSP: Safety-guaranteed Surgical Policy via Joint Optimization of Behavioral and Spatial Constraints
institution_matches.overview: Introduces the SSP framework, which wraps learned surgical policies (RL, IL, CLF-based) with a Control Barrier Function safety controller derived from a Neural ODE dynamics model. The controller enforces both behavioral and spatial constraints, achieving near-zero safety violations on SurRoL and dVRK while maintaining high task success, pointing to a practical route for safe autonomous surgical manipulation.

decision_card.core_claim: SSP learns an uncertainty-aware dynamics model with Neural ODEs to build a robust CBF-based safety controller that minimally modifies surgical robot policies while enforcing behavioral and spatial constraints with near-zero violations.
decision_card.priority_reason: Addresses safety for data-driven surgical manipulation; methods extend to constrained manipulation with formal guarantees.

institution_matches.title: Foundational World Models Accurately Detect Bimanual Manipulator Failures
institution_matches.overview: Trains a probabilistic world model in the latent space of a large vision tokenizer and uses its uncertainty within a conformal prediction framework as a runtime monitor for bimanual manipulation. On a new Bimanual Cable Manipulation dataset and Push-T, it outperforms anomaly/OOD baselines with 20× fewer learnable parameters than the next best method, enabling efficient failure detection in high-dimensional manipulation systems.

decision_card.core_claim: A probabilistic world model trained in the latent space of a vision foundation model provides uncertainty estimates that, via conformal prediction, enable runtime monitoring and accurate failure detection for bimanual manipulation tasks at lower parameter cost than baselines.
decision_card.risk_note: Evaluations on a specific simulated task and one real-world dataset; broader coverage unclear.
decision_card.priority_reason: Directly targets failure detection for bimanual manipulation using foundation-model latents; important safety component for real deployments.

institution_matches.title: Unified Structural-Hydrodynamic Modeling of Underwater Underactuated Mechanisms and Soft Robots
institution_matches.overview: Proposes a CMA-ES–inspired trajectory-level identification framework that jointly estimates structural and hydrodynamic parameters for underwater underactuated and soft robots. Demonstrated on a multibody mechanism and an octopus-inspired soft-arm robot, the method attains <5% end-effector error and transfers across platforms without retuning, supporting higher-fidelity planning and control in underwater manipulation scenarios.

decision_card.core_claim: This work presents a trajectory-driven global optimization framework to jointly identify structural and hydrodynamic parameters for underwater underactuated and soft robots, achieving low trajectory errors and enabling transfer from mechanisms to a soft octopus robot without retuning.
decision_card.priority_reason: High-fidelity modeling for underwater robots; only indirectly connected to mainstream terrestrial manipulation research.

institution_matches.title: Residual Control for Fast Recovery from Dynamics Shifts
institution_matches.overview: Treats dynamics changes at deployment as disturbance shaping around a frozen nominal RL policy, adding a bounded residual channel regulated by a Stability Alignment Gate. Across quadruped, biped, humanoid, and wheeled robots, this residual scheme cuts recovery time from mid-episode perturbations by 20–87% while preserving near-nominal steady-state behavior, offering a generic robustness layer for existing controllers.

decision_card.core_claim: Residual Control with a Stability Alignment Gate adds a bounded, aligned residual policy on top of a nominal controller to compensate for unobserved dynamics shifts online, reducing recovery time across several robot platforms without retraining.
decision_card.risk_note: Reported results are on simulated platforms; no real-robot data in abstract.
decision_card.priority_reason: Generic adaptation strategy valuable for robustness to dynamics shifts, including in manipulators, though examples focus on locomotion robots.

institution_matches.title: Energy-Efficient Collaborative Transport of Tether-Suspended Payloads via Rotating Equilibrium
institution_matches.overview: Analyzes and demonstrates a rotating-equilibrium strategy for multi-UAV transport of tether-suspended payloads, where centrifugal forces supply horizontal tension while each vehicle produces purely vertical thrust. Experiments show up to 20% power savings over static lifting, expanding the practical envelope for aerial cooperative transport missions.

decision_card.core_claim: Operating multi-UAV tethered-payload systems at a rotating equilibrium leverages centrifugal forces so each vehicle can use nearly vertical thrust, reducing power consumption by up to 20% compared to static lifting.
decision_card.risk_note: Very specific aerial transport setting; connection to general manipulation limited.
decision_card.priority_reason: Niche aerial cooperative transport result; limited overlap with core manipulation or VLA topics.

institution_matches.title: Receding-Horizon Nullspace Optimization for Actuation-Aware Control Allocation in Omnidirectional UAVs
institution_matches.overview: Presents a receding-horizon allocation scheme for fully actuated omnidirectional UAVs that explicitly models asymmetric motor dynamics and uses nullspace optimization to smooth motor commands. Using constrained iLQR over a prediction horizon, the method reduces oscillations and improves 6-DoF trajectory tracking compared to standard single-step QP allocation.

decision_card.core_claim: This paper proposes a receding-horizon control allocation method for omnidirectional UAVs that accounts for asymmetric motor dynamics via nullspace optimization, reducing motor oscillations and improving trajectory tracking versus conventional QP allocation.
decision_card.risk_note: Evidence appears simulation-only for a specific UAV platform.
decision_card.priority_reason: Primarily aerial control allocation; little direct impact on manipulation or embodied AI at present.

institution_matches.title: Learning-Based Robust Control: Unifying Exploration and Distributional Robustness for Reliable Robotics via Free Energy
institution_matches.overview: Builds a free-energy–motivated framework that jointly learns dynamics and reward models while enforcing distributional robustness to epistemic uncertainty. On continuous control benchmarks and Franka tabletop manipulation, the approach narrows the sim-to-real gap and delivers repeatable performance without task-specific fine-tuning, highlighting a principled route to robust learned controllers.

decision_card.core_claim: The paper introduces a free-energy-based learning framework that jointly learns dynamics and rewards while ensuring distributional robustness to epistemic uncertainty, narrowing sim-to-real gaps and enabling repeatable Franka arm manipulation without task-specific fine-tuning.
decision_card.risk_note: Scope of tasks and comparative baselines not clear in abstract.
decision_card.priority_reason: Conceptually interesting robust-control framework validated on manipulation; could inform safer, more reliable RL-based controllers.

institution_matches.title: Toward Global Intent Inference for Human Motion by Inverse Reinforcement Learning
institution_matches.overview: Uses Minimal Observation IRL with a rich set of candidate cost terms to infer time-varying global cost functions for planar human reaching motions. A single subject- and posture-agnostic cost with dynamically changing weights reconstructs trajectories well, supporting the hypothesis of unified underlying optimality principles relevant for human-robot interaction modeling.

decision_card.risk_note: Conservative fallback due to incomplete model output.
decision_card.priority_reason: Fallback score from topic relevance.

institution_matches.title: Towards Scalable Probabilistic Human Motion Prediction with Gaussian Processes for Safe Human-Robot Collaboration
institution_matches.overview: Introduces a structured multitask variational GP model for full-body human motion prediction with calibrated uncertainty and 6D rotation representation. On Human3.6M it achieves strong probabilistic accuracy with ~0.24–0.35M parameters and modest inference times, yielding interpretable uncertainty suitable for safe HRI planning and collision avoidance.

decision_card.risk_note: Conservative fallback due to incomplete model output.
decision_card.priority_reason: Fallback score from topic relevance.

topic_matches.title: MetaWorld-X: Hierarchical World Modeling via VLM-Orchestrated Experts for Humanoid Loco-Manipulation
topic_matches.institution: University of Alberta; University of Science and Technology of China
topic_matches.overview: Proposes a hierarchical world-model framework for humanoid loco-manipulation that decomposes control into Specialized Expert Policies trained with human-motion priors for biomechanically natural behavior. A Vision-Language–supervised routing mechanism composes experts based on task semantics, enabling stable, natural whole-body control and compositional generalization across multi-stage tasks.

decision_card.core_claim: MetaWorld-X decomposes humanoid loco-manipulation into imitation-constrained expert policies orchestrated by a VLM-supervised router to achieve more natural, stable, and compositionally generalizable whole-body control.
decision_card.risk_note: Abstract emphasizes capabilities but provides no quantitative details or breakdown of real vs sim performance.
decision_card.priority_reason: Directly combines VLM routing with hierarchical control for humanoid loco-manipulation, highly aligned with long-horizon, semantic skill composition research.

topic_matches.title: Multifingered force-aware control for humanoid robots
topic_matches.overview: Develops a model-based whole-body controller for humanoids with multifingered hands that redistributes contact forces using estimated fingertip forces from tactile sensors. By minimizing the distance between the center of pressure and the fingertips’ contact polygon centroid, the method stabilizes objects of varying mass distributions in balancing and multi-object scenarios.

decision_card.core_claim: This work trains tactile-force estimators from magnetic tactile sensors and uses a model-based controller to redistribute fingertip forces via whole-body motion to maintain stable object contact with a humanoid’s multifingered hand.
decision_card.risk_note: Validated on limited object set and specific balancing tasks.
decision_card.priority_reason: Useful force-distribution control scheme using tactile sensing on humanoids; moderate scope but relevant for dexterous stability and whole-body manipulation.

topic_matches.title: EquiBim: Learning Symmetry-Equivariant Policy for Bimanual Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: Introduces EquiBim, a model-agnostic framework that enforces bilateral symmetry equivariance between observations and actions in dual-arm systems via group-action constraints. Applied across point-cloud and image-based policies and different action spaces on the RoboTwin platform, it yields more consistent and robust bimanual manipulation under distribution shifts.

decision_card.core_claim: EquiBim introduces a symmetry-equivariant policy learning framework for dual-arm robots that enforces bilateral equivariance between observations and actions, improving performance and robustness in bimanual manipulation.
decision_card.priority_reason: Clean inductive bias (equivariance) with both sim and hardware validation for bimanual tasks; method is model-agnostic and likely synergistic with existing imitation/VLA pipelines.

topic_matches.title: MoMaStage: Skill-State Graph Guided Planning and Closed-Loop Execution for Long-Horizon Indoor Mobile Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: Presents MoMaStage, which grounds a VLM into a hierarchical skill library and a topology-aware Skill-State Graph to constrain task decomposition and sequencing for indoor mobile manipulation. Closed-loop execution with graph-constrained replanning maintains logical and topological consistency, improving long-horizon success and token efficiency in simulation and real environments.

decision_card.core_claim: MoMaStage uses a VLM grounded in a hierarchical skill library and a topology-aware skill-state graph plus closed-loop semantic replanning to improve long-horizon indoor mobile manipulation success without explicit mapping.
decision_card.risk_note: Evaluated on specific indoor MoMA setups; generality beyond tested domains unclear.
decision_card.priority_reason: Strongly aligned with graph-structured planning plus VLM grounding for mobile manipulation; promising for long-horizon task execution with fewer tokens.

topic_matches.title: Viewpoint-Agnostic Grasp Pipeline using VLM and Partial Observations
topic_matches.overview: Proposes a language-guided grasping pipeline for a legged mobile manipulator that combines open-vocabulary detection and segmentation with depth compensation and two-stage point-cloud completion. It then generates and safety-filters 6-DoF grasps, achieving 90% success in cluttered scenes versus 30% for a view-dependent baseline, highlighting robustness to occlusion and partial views.

decision_card.core_claim: This work presents a language-guided grasping pipeline for legged mobile manipulators that combines open-vocabulary segmentation, depth compensation, point-cloud completion, and safety-aware grasp filtering to achieve high success in clutter under occlusion.
decision_card.risk_note: Evaluated on two scenarios and a specific quadruped platform.
decision_card.priority_reason: Well-engineered end-to-end grasp pipeline for mobile manipulation; method components are modular and directly deployable.

topic_matches.title: Two-Stage Path Following for Mobile Manipulators via Dimensionality-Reduced Graph Search and Numerical Optimization
topic_matches.institution: Authors are with College of Mechanical and Vehicle Engineering, ChongQing University
topic_matches.overview: Introduces a two-stage planner for mobile manipulators that first solves a reduced 2-DoF base-planning problem via IRM-based multi-layer graph search, then refines it with continuous L-BFGS optimization. The approach enforces reachability and yields sub-millimeter tracking accuracy in simulation and robust path following on an omnidirectional mobile manipulator.

decision_card.core_claim: This paper proposes a two-stage path-following planner for mobile manipulators that first finds a base path via Dijkstra on an IRM-discretized graph, then refines it with continuous optimization to achieve smooth, kinematically accurate trajectories under reachability constraints.
decision_card.priority_reason: Solid planning contribution for mobile manipulators; method is classical/optimization-centric and complementary to learning-based approaches.

topic_matches.title: PhaForce: Phase-Scheduled Visual-Force Policy Learning with Slow Planning and Fast Correction for Contact-Rich Manipulation
topic_matches.overview: Combines a contact-aware phase predictor, a low-frequency diffusion planner with dual-gated visual–force fusion, and a high-rate residual corrector to handle contact-rich manipulation. PhaForce explicitly schedules how and when force feedback influences control, achieving 86% success and substantially improved contact quality on real-robot tasks versus vision-only baselines.

decision_card.core_claim: PhaForce introduces a phase-scheduled visual–force policy that couples slow diffusion-based planning with fast residual correction routed by contact-aware phase estimates, yielding large gains on real-robot contact-rich tasks.
decision_card.priority_reason: Directly tackles force-aware, contact-rich manipulation with a clear architecture for slow/fast control; strong real-robot improvements make it highly actionable.

topic_matches.title: Embedding Classical Balance Control Principles in Reinforcement Learning for Humanoid Recovery
topic_matches.overview: Trains a unified RL policy for humanoid disturbance rejection and recovery by feeding classical balance metrics (capture point, CoM state, centroidal momentum) to the critic while keeping the actor proprioceptive-only. Without scripted references, the learned controller covers ankle/hip strategies, stepping, and multi-contact stand-up, achieving over 93% recovery across diverse falls.

decision_card.core_claim: Embedding classical balance metrics as privileged critic inputs and reward terms enables a single RL policy to perform disturbance rejection, stepping, and multi-contact stand-up for humanoids with high recovery rates and promising sim-to-real transfer.
decision_card.risk_note: Only "preliminary" hardware tests are mentioned; majority of evidence from sim.
decision_card.priority_reason: Humanoid balance and recovery are important for loco-manipulation platforms, but this is mostly locomotion-focused rather than manipulation-centric.

topic_matches.title: Towards Human-Like Manipulation through RL-Augmented Teleoperation and Mixture-of-Dexterous-Experts VLA
topic_matches.institution: National University of Singapore; Shanghai Jiao Tong University
topic_matches.overview: Introduces IMCopilot, a set of RL-trained atomic in-hand manipulation skills that assist teleoperation and serve as low-level primitives, and MoDE-VLA, a mixture-of-dexterous-experts VLA integrating force and tactile sensing via residual injection. On challenging bimanual in-hand tasks, the framework roughly doubles success rates over a baseline VLA, pushing VLA use into high-DOF dexterous regimes.

decision_card.core_claim: The framework combines RL-trained atomic skills for shared-autonomy teleoperation (IMCopilot) with a Mixture-of-Dexterous-Experts VLA that fuses force and tactile modalities via residual injection, doubling success rates on challenging dexterous tasks.
decision_card.risk_note: Data scale, task diversity, and teleoperation protocol details are not fully specified in abstract.
decision_card.priority_reason: Targets human-like bimanual dexterous manipulation with VLAs and RL-augmented data collection; strategically important albeit early-stage.

topic_matches.title: Low-Cost Teleoperation Extension for Mobile Manipulators
topic_matches.overview: Presents an open-source teleoperation system for mobile bimanual manipulators that leverages smartphone-based head tracking, leader arms, and foot pedals for simultaneous whole-body control. User studies show better task performance and lower cognitive load versus keyboard control, offering an accessible interface for data collection and remote mobile manipulation.

decision_card.core_claim: This work provides a low-cost teleoperation framework for mobile bimanual manipulators using smartphone head tracking, leader arms, and foot pedals, showing improved task performance and reduced cognitive load versus keyboard control.
decision_card.risk_note: User studies likely on limited tasks and users; generalization to more complex operations unclear.
decision_card.priority_reason: Useful teleop interface for data collection and supervision of mobile manipulators; more systems than algorithms.
